title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol Using TRIzol LS Reagent
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4rwgv7e
materials: Thaw and aliquot homogenate into several Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at lowest speed to cause algae to form pellet
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at lowest speed to cause algae to form pellet.

steps.title: Homogenize each 250 µL portion of...
steps.instruction: Homogenize each 250 µL portion of pellet material to a powder in liquid nitrogen using mortar and pestle prechilled with liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Add 750 µL TRIzol LS to...
steps.instruction: Add 750 µL TRIzol LS to each 250 µL of homogenized algal material.

steps.title: Homogenization is continued until the TRIzol is pulverized as well
steps.instruction: Homogenization is continued until the TRIzol is pulverized as well.

steps.title: Add 50 µL potassium acetate ( 0
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL potassium acetate ( 0.2 Molarity (M) final concentration) to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C .

steps.title: Add 200 µL chloroform (for polysaccharide-rich...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL chloroform (for polysaccharide-rich algae), or 100 µL BCP to each sample.

steps.title: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 20 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: RNA will remain in the upper, aqueous phase (ca
steps.instruction: RNA will remain in the upper, aqueous phase (ca. 70 % of the applied TRIzol).

steps.title: Carefully transfer each RNA phase into RNase-free 1
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer each RNA phase into RNase-free 1.5 ml tubes.

steps.title: Add 500 µL isopropanol
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL isopropanol.

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at -20 °C .

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Wash pellet with 75 % ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash pellet with 75 % ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Repeat ethanol wash steps
steps.instruction: Repeat ethanol wash steps. go to step #17

steps.title: Dry pellet at 50 °C for...
steps.instruction: Dry pellet at 50 °C for 00:05:00 – 00:10:00 Appearance of drying pellet is important: drying should be terminated when the pellet begins to become transparent; contaminated RNA remains white)

steps.title: Add RNAse-free water
steps.instruction: Add RNAse-free water.

steps.title: Dissolve pellet completely by pipetting
steps.instruction: Dissolve pellet completely by pipetting.

steps.title: Wash several times with sterile culture medium (not DEPC-treated)
steps.instruction: Wash several times with sterile culture medium (not DEPC-treated).

steps.title: After washing, the algal material is...
steps.instruction: After washing, the algal material is aliquoted into portions of 250 µL (ca. 50 mg – 100 mg packed cell volume).

steps.title: Add more nitrogen if needed (see...
steps.instruction: Add more nitrogen if needed (see also Procedure described in Protocol 9).

steps.title: Shake samples for 00:00:15
steps.instruction: Shake samples for 00:00:15 .

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C – 60 °C for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C – 60 °C for 00:10:00 .
